title: Verify email — no token: 'Invalid or missing verification link.' shown with Go to Login button

preconditions.description: Expo web app is running and reachable at the configured base URL.

journey.description: Navigate to verify-email without any token query parameter. useLocalSearchParams returns undefined for 'token'.
No-token state is rendered immediately (no API call is made).

assertions.description: The no-token (and invalid) state renders the 'Invalid or missing verification link.' heading.
Go to Login button is visible in invalid/no-token state.
No loading state appears — status is set to 'no-token' synchronously on mount without making an API call.
No email input appears (email input is only shown in 'expired' state, not 'no-token').
Tapping Go to Login navigates to login screen.

manualValidationRequired.reason: No supported native automation runner is currently configured.
No supported native automation runner is currently configured.

notes: This test does not require a running backend — the no-token case is handled entirely on the client side in useEffect: if (!token) { setStatus('no-token'); return; }
The 'no-token' and 'invalid' states share the same JSX render block and display the same text. This test covers navigation to the route without a token query param.
requiredBackend is false because no API call is triggered when token is absent.
